name: Java HashMap Internals
chatResponse: I'll visualize the internal lifecycle of a `put(K, V)` operation in a HashMap, illustrating how it handles hashing, collisions, and performance optimizations like treeification.

steps.title: Hashing and Index Calculation
steps.content: When you call `put(key, value)`, Java first computes the key's `hashCode()`. To prevent poor hash functions from causing collisions, the `HashMap` applies a supplementary hash function. It then calculates the specific bucket index using the formula: `index = (n - 1) & hash`, where `n` is the current capacity of the internal array (table).

steps.title: Bucket Assignment & Collision Handling
steps.content: If the calculated bucket is empty, a new node is created. If a collision occurs (the bucket is already occupied), the `HashMap` checks if the key already exists by comparing both the hash and the `.equals()` method. If the key is the same, the value is updated. If the keys are different, the entry is added to a linked structure within that bucket.

steps.title: LinkedList vs. Red-Black Tree
steps.content: Since Java 8, if a single bucket becomes too crowded (threshold of 8 elements), the `HashMap` converts the internal LinkedList into a **Red-Black Tree** (TreeNodes). This improves the worst-case search time from $O(n)$ to $O(\log n)$. If the bucket size decreases later (threshold of 6), it converts back to a simple list.

visualCode:
graph TD
    Start([put key, value]) --> Hash[Calculate hash = hash\(key\)]
    Hash --> Index[Index = hash & n-1]
    Index --> Lookup{Bucket Empty?}
    Lookup -- Yes --> Create[Create New Node]
    Lookup -- No --> Compare{Key exists?}
    Compare -- Yes / equals == true --> Update[Update Existing Value]
    Compare -- No / equals == false --> CheckType{Bucket Type?}
    CheckType -- LinkedList --> AppendLL[Append to Tail of List]
    CheckType -- TreeNode --> InsertTree[Insert into Red-Black Tree]
    AppendLL --> TreeCheck{Length >= 8?}
    TreeCheck -- Yes --> Treeify[Convert List to TreeNode]
    TreeCheck -- No --> End([Operation Complete])
    Create --> End
    Update --> End
    Treeify --> End
    InsertTree --> End
